data.title: Sivers Semiconductors appoints Heine Thorsgaard as new CFO to enhance growth capabilities. (Characters: 91)
data.description: Sivers Semiconductors AB, based in KISTA, Sweden, has appointed Heine Thorsgaard as the new Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to enhance capabilities for future growth. The company, known for its expertise in photonics and wireless technologies, made the critical move on August 8, 2025. Sivers Semiconductors AB, listed as SIVE.ST, is a prominent player in the global market.

data.title: Wall Street predicts Wendy's Q2 earnings at 25 cents/share, down from 27 cents last year. Revenue expected at $558.03 million, down from $570.73 million. Wendy's shares fell 0.8% to close at $9.96 ahead of earnings report.
data.description: The Wendy's Company (NASDAQ: WEN) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings before the market opens on Friday, Aug. 8. Analysts are predicting earnings of 25 cents per share for the Dublin-based fast-food chain, a decline from 27 cents per share in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is forecasted to be $558.03 million, down from $570.73 million in the previous year. Wendy's has fallen short of analyst expectations in six out of the last 10 quarters, including the first quarter. The company's stock closed at $9.96 on Thursday, showing a 0.8% decrease. For more details and access to the latest analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga Pro platform.

data.title: Two individuals arrested for throwing sex toys at WNBA games as part of memecoin prank trend.
data.description: A group associated with a memecoin acknowledged responsibility for the recent trend where sex toys are being tossed during WNBA games. This trend has led to the arrest of two individuals who are accused of participating in these incidents.

data.title: Sai Life Sciences Limited's Q1FY26 Revenue grows by 77% YoY to ₹496 Cr; EBITDA ₹125 Cr; Net Profit ₹60 Cr.
data.description: In the first quarter of the fiscal year 2026, the revenue stood at Rs. 496 crore, EBITDA reached Rs. 125 crore, and net profit amounted to Rs. 60 crore.

data.title: Nxera Pharma reports Q2 and first half 2025 results. Tokyo, Japan & Cambridge, UK. TSE: 4565. Update on operational activities.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, Nxera Pharma, a pharmaceutical company based in Tokyo, Japan and Cambridge, UK, shared details about its operations and released its financial results for the second quarter and first half of 2025. The company, listed as TSE: 4565, unveiled the information in a comprehensive report accessible through their platform.

data.title: Block Inc. CEO credits AI for growth, raises full-year profit guidance to $10.17 billion due to faster product launches.
data.description: Fintech company Block Inc. (NYSE: XYZ) raised its full-year profit guidance to $10.17 billion during the second quarter 2025 earnings call, despite underwhelming performance. CEO Jack Dorsey credited the company's growth and product speed to the integration of artificial intelligence. Dorsey expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction, stating optimism towards future endeavors. He emphasized a commitment to faster innovation, highlighting the successful launch of new products such as Cash App Pools within three months. Dorsey's bullish outlook stems from Block's accelerated development and dedication to enhancing user experience.

data.title: Deloitte India partners with Embark to support Global Capability Centres, aiding global firms in India.
data.description: In Bengaluru, India on August 8, 2025, Deloitte India revealed a new partnership with Embark, a unified platform for Global Capability Centres (GCCs). This collaboration aims to provide extensive assistance to international companies in setting up and expanding their GCCs in India. Through this alliance, a wide range of services will be offered...

data.title: Tempus AI to release Q2 earnings on Aug 8. Analysts predict net loss of 25 cents/share and revenue of $297.76 million. gained 5.8%, closing at $58.74.
data.description: Tempus AI, Inc. (NASDAQ: TEM) is scheduled to unveil its Q2 earnings before the market opens on Friday, August 8th. Analysts are predicting a net loss of 25 cents per share for the Chicago-based company, as opposed to a loss of 63 cents per share in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is projected to be approximately $297.76 million, marking a substantial increase from $165.97 million reported in the previous year. Noteworthy to mention, on July 16, Tempus AI secured 510(k) clearance from the FDA for its ejection fraction software. Following this news, Tempus AI's stock saw a 5.8% rise, closing at $58.74 on Thursday. For further information, readers can refer to the detailed story on their website.
